notes: Hand-written clues, 4 per country, ordered hardest/most obscure -> most obvious. Country names must match the Natural Earth (world-atlas) names used as keys in the DATA object in index.html; aliases for duplicate-named countries (Czechia/Czech Rep., etc.) get their own entry. To add a continent, append its batch entries to the countries array -- any country without an entry here is drawn on the map but never used as a quiz target.

clues: Its capital was carved out of land donated by two states and belongs to no state at all.
It's home to the world's first national park, established in 1872.
It's the third-largest country in the world by population.
Its flag has 50 stars, one for each state.
It has more lakes than the rest of the world's lakes combined.
Its capital was chosen partly because it sat safely between two rival cities, Toronto and Quebec City.
It's the second-largest country in the world by area, despite a relatively small population.
Its flag features a maple leaf.
Its capital was built atop the ruins of the Aztec city of Tenochtitlan.
It gave the world chocolate, chili peppers, and corn.
It has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country in the Americas.
Its flag features an eagle eating a snake atop a cactus.
It's home to Tikal, one of the largest ancient Maya cities ever discovered.
It's one of the world's top exporters of cardamom, a spice more associated with India.
Its name is thought to come from a Nahuatl word meaning 'place of many trees.'
It's the most populous country in Central America.
It's the largest island in the Caribbean.
Classic American cars from the 1950s are still commonly driven here today.
It's known worldwide for cigars and son-based music styles that shaped salsa.
It lies just about 90 miles south of Florida.
Its name is Spanish for 'depths,' possibly referring to deep coastal waters Columbus encountered.
It's home to the ancient Maya ruins of Copán.
It has coastline on the Caribbean Sea but not the Pacific Ocean mainland proper.

It contains about 5% of the world's biodiversity despite its small size.

It's the only place in the world where you can see the sun rise on the Pacific and set on the Atlantic from certain points.
